reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal statement. `ZMod p` with `Nat.Prime p` correctly encodes F = ℤ_p; `Polynomial (ZMod p)` is F[x]; `Irreducible q` and `q.degree = n` capture "q irreducible of degree n"; and `Polynomial (ZMod p) ⧸ span {q}` (with `open Ideal`) is F[x]/(q(x)). The conclusion is the conjunction of (i) existence of a Fintype instance whose cardinality equals p^n, and (ii) `IsField` on the quotient — precisely "field with exactly p^n elements". The existential-Fintype form is a standard Mathlib way to assert finite cardinality. Irreducibility forces q ≠ 0 and non-unit (so deg q ≥ 1), so no degenerate case is smuggled in. No hypotheses are dropped or added beyond the informal claim.
